title: Ensembles and Cocktails: Robust Finetuning for Natural Language Generation

structured.summary: This paper proposes StyleTunedLM, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach using LoRA adapters to customize large language model generation styles to match specific authors. It demonstrates improved stylistic fidelity and instruction-following abilities via adapter merging, outperforming few-shot and instruction-based baselines on multiple automatic metrics.

strengths.point: Effective style adaptation with parameter-efficient fine-tuning
strengths.evidence: StyleTunedLM reduces perplexity up to 13.6% and achieves higher style embedding similarity and classification accuracy compared to few-shot and instruction baselines.

strengths.point: Novel merging of LoRA adapters enabling simultaneous style control and instruction adherence
strengths.evidence: The paper introduces concatenation and weighted merging of style and instruction LoRA modules, maintaining instruction-following ability while improving stylistic fidelity.

weaknesses.point: Limited comparison to recent style transfer methods
weaknesses.evidence: The evaluation omits comparison to other recent style control approaches using pretrained models with explicit control tokens or prefix tuning, limiting claims of novelty.

weaknesses.point: Lack of human evaluation and limited qualitative analysis
weaknesses.evidence: No human judgments on fluency or style authenticity are reported, and only a small number of example generations are provided, constraining assessment of real-world generation quality.

questions.question: How does StyleTunedLM perform on low-resource or out-of-distribution authors?
questions.motivation: Evaluating on unseen or culturally diverse authors is critical to understand generalizability beyond canonical authors likely included in pretraining.

questions.question: What is the sensitivity of style adaptation to LoRA hyperparameter choices?
questions.motivation: Ablation on LoRA rank and scaling would clarify robustness and whether improvements rely on careful hyperparameter tuning.

critique_points.point_001: Limited comparison to recent style transfer methods. The evaluation omits comparison to other recent style control approaches using pretrained models with explicit control tokens or prefix tuning, limiting claims of novelty
critique_points.point_002: Lack of human evaluation and limited qualitative analysis. No human judgments on fluency or style authenticity are reported, and only a small number of example generations are provided, constraining assessment of real-world generation quality
